hypothesis: Fine-tuning a domain-specific language model on MatPROV's PROV-DM graph structures will yield higher structural-level F1 scores than the best-performing prompted general-purpose model (o4-mini with one-shot prompting, F1 = 0.835) when evaluated on synthesis procedures from material classes underrepresented in the training corpus (e.g., polymers or ceramics), because explicit supervision on the graph-theoretic relationships between entities, activities, and parameters will generalize more robustly across material domains than in-context example selection alone.
